3.happening: We record n, the number of elements.
3.why: Both passes iterate over exactly n indices.
3.invariant: n is the length of nums.

4.happening: We build answer as a list of n ones.
4.why: Starting from one lets us multiply products in without special cases.
4.invariant: answer currently holds a neutral value in every cell.

5.happening: The running prefix product starts at one.
5.why: Before index 0 there are no elements to the left, so the product is one.
5.invariant: prefix equals the product of all elements strictly left of index i.

6.happening: The pointer i sweeps forward to the next index.
6.why: The forward pass fills each cell with the product of everything to its left.
6.invariant: Cells before i already hold their left-side products.

7.happening: We write the current prefix into answer[{i}].
7.why: answer[i] should equal the product of all elements to the left of i.
7.invariant: answer[i] now equals the product of elements strictly left of i.

8.why: The next cell needs a prefix that includes the current element.
8.invariant: prefix now equals the product of all elements up to and including i.

9.happening: The running suffix product starts at one.
9.why: Past the last index there are no elements to the right, so the product is one.
9.invariant: suffix equals the product of all elements strictly right of i.

10.happening: The pointer i sweeps backward to the next index.
10.why: The backward pass multiplies in the product of everything to each cell's right.
10.invariant: Cells after i already hold their finished products.

11.happening: We multiply answer[{i}] by the running suffix.
11.why: Combining the stored left product with the right product gives the answer for i.
11.invariant: answer[i] now equals the product of every element except nums[i].

12.why: The next cell to the left needs a suffix that includes the current element.
12.invariant: suffix now equals the product of all elements from i to the end.

13.why: Both passes are done, so every cell holds its final product.
13.invariant: answer[i] equals the product of all elements except nums[i] for every i.

init.happening: Prepare the length, the all-ones answer array, and the prefix product.
init.why: The two passes build on a neutral starting array.
init.invariant: No products have been folded in yet.

loop.why: One pass runs left to right, the other right to left.
loop.invariant: Every index is visited exactly once per pass.

update.happening: Write a product into a cell or fold a value into a running product.
update.why: Each step contributes one factor to the prefix or suffix.
update.invariant: answer and the running products always reflect the elements processed so far.

return.invariant: Every cell holds the product of all other elements.
